name: Jackson County Co-Op
description_short: Jackson County Federal Credit Union is a member-owned financial institution in Edna, Texas offering checking, savings, certificates, and consumer loans with competitive rates and local service.
description_long:
Jackson County Federal Credit Union (JCFCU) is a federally chartered credit union based in Edna, Texas, serving members in Jackson County and surrounding areas. The institution operates as a cooperative financial institution where members are also owners, distinguishing it from traditional banks. JCFCU has been providing financial services to its community with a focus on personalized banking solutions tailored to individual member needs.

The credit union offers a comprehensive suite of banking products including Share Draft accounts (checking with monthly dividends), Regular and Special Share accounts (savings with quarterly dividends), and Regular Certificates of Deposit with flexible dividend options. On the lending side, JCFCU provides auto loans for new and used vehicles, boat and RV financing, unsecured personal loans, and share-secured loans. Members can access these services through the main lobby (Monday-Friday, 9 a.m.-4 p.m.) or drive-through windows with extended Thursday-Friday hours (8:30 a.m.-5 p.m.).

As a credit union rather than a commercial bank, JCFCU emphasizes member benefits including competitive rates, low fees, and personalized local service. The institution provides multiple contact channels including phone banking (Audio Response/Teller-Anytime at 888-286-3735), dedicated fraud support lines, and email communication. Their membership model means profits are returned to members through competitive dividend rates and lower loan rates compared to traditional banks.

JCFCU is appropriate for individuals seeking community-based banking with a personal touch, though as a regional credit union with a single physical location in Edna, Texas, membership eligibility and service area may be limited compared to national banks. The institution's website emphasizes membership accessibility and benefits, though specific membership requirements and eligibility criteria are not fully detailed on the public pages reviewed.
services: Share Draft accounts (checking with monthly dividends)
Boat and RV loans (new and used)
pros: Member-owned cooperative structure returns profits through competitive dividend rates on savings accounts
Offers both new and used auto/boat/RV loans plus unsecured personal and share-secured loans
Extended drive-through hours (until 5 p.m. Thursday-Friday) beyond standard lobby hours for working members
Multiple account types (checking, savings, certificates) with stated competitive rates
Dedicated customer support lines for fraud/lost cards (800-610-2696) and audio banking (888-286-3735)
Local, in-person service with emphasis on personalized financial solutions and relationship banking
ABA routing number provided (313189715) enabling direct deposit and transfers
cons: Single physical location in Edna, Texas may limit accessibility for members outside Jackson County area
Limited information on membership eligibility criteria and service area boundaries on public website
No mention of online banking, mobile app, or digital services beyond audio response system
Relatively small institution with fewer product offerings compared to regional or national credit unions
No stated information about ATM network access or surcharge-free ATM availability
best_for: Jackson County, Texas residents seeking relationship-based banking from a local credit union
Members wanting to finance auto, boat, or RV purchases with competitive rates
Savers looking for dividend-earning checking and savings accounts through a cooperative institution
diagnosis: Jackson County Federal Credit Union is best suited for residents of Jackson County, Texas seeking personalized, relationship-based banking with competitive rates through a member-owned cooperative. The primary caveat is the institution's single-location footprint in Edna, Texas, which may limit accessibility and service convenience for members outside the immediate area, and the apparent lack of digital banking platforms limits remote account management options.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
